ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: U nan dek lipuul le ki ma ubijabo. Le u bui ke, “Uwumbɔr chuu nyan maanimɔɔn le ki tii mi ubijabo.
translated: You should go to the forest and bring me a monkey. And you should say, "God has given me wisdom and made me a monkey.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: ki cha bi chuun iwiin itaa ki buen timoor ni, nin chee daa na. Le Jakob nan gaa Labann aapiih i gur na ki joo i.
translated: They were still talking with them when Rachel arrived with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess. When Jacob saw Rachel, Laban's daughter, he went and rolled the stone away.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Le Jakob ŋun ke Labann aajapɔtiib bui ke, “Jakob gaa tite aawan mɔmɔk a. Waah kpa tiwan nimɔk na nyan ni tite yaan ponn ni la.”
translated: When Jacob heard Laban's sons' words saying, "Jacob has taken all our father's wealth. And he has acquired all this wealth from what belonged to our father."

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Baah fuu ni na le u bui bi ke, “M bee ke nite aa ki gee maabɔr ke waah nan gee mi njan pu na; tɔ, nte Aawumbɔr bi m chee.
translated: If anyone asks you why you are doing this, tell them, "The Lord needs it and will return it soon"; or, "The Lord has sent me."

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jakob nan ŋmann Labann, le kaa cha u nyi ke u ga nya udo.
translated: Jacob stayed with Laban, and they agreed that he would work for him for seven years.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Aa mu yaa kan unii u joo saawaa na kan, ku udaan ngbaan. Ti mɔmɔk sɔ do, timi aanib bee le ga li ye biseeraadam, lik m ni maanib aawan mɔmɔk ponn ni, ki nyan saawan aah ye ni na mɔmɔk.” Jakob aa nan nyi ke upuu Rachel le sun ute Labann aawaa.
translated: If you will go with me to my father's house, I will give you a great reward. We will agree together, you and I, that you will serve me for seven years, and I will give you my younger daughter to marry, the one you love." Jacob agreed to this with Laban's daughter Rachel whom he loved.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Labann nan bui Jakob ke, “Mpiibim bimina ye mbisatiib la, le min le yeh baabim ni tiwakor timina mɔmɔk. Mbamɔn, tiwan nimɔk bi do na ye maawan la. Le maah kaan ŋmaa gaa mbisatiib ni baabim na kan,
translated: Laban said to Jacob, "Because you are my relative, should you work for me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be. Now, I have two daughters. The older one is called Leah and the younger one is Rachel.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Labann nan ki bui ke, “Aa yaa ŋa mbisatiib bee bakaa, bee aa yaa ki yoor bipiib kpee bi pu kan, hali m yaa kaa nyi kan, teer ke Uwumbɔr waa si.
translated: "The people said to him, "If you are the Christ or the king, come down from the cross and save yourself, and if you do that, then we will believe that God sent you."

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Waah nan di bi puur doo na, le u ki di waawangol mɔmɔk mu di puur,
translated: When they are about to harvest, they don't allow weeds to grow in the farm,

translated: The man asked him, "What is your name?" And he answered, "Jacob."

translated: "If you ask him, he will say, 'Yes, the flocks belong to your servant Jacob, and he is sending them as a gift to you.'" Jacob then said, "I will appease Esau with the gift that goes before me, and afterward when I see his face, perhaps he will accept me."

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Le nwiin aah puu buyoonn na Jakob nyan Peniel ki cha, le ki nan deeni ukpiik pu.

translated: He told his father, "Father, the firstborn of Jacob is the one who is supposed to eat it." Jacob then answered, "My brother Esau is a hairy man, but I am a smooth man."

translated: And Esau said, "What is the meaning of all this company that I met?" And Jacob said, "To find favor in the sight of my lord."
